overview: Add an in-app provider configuration dialog that lowers API setup friction while preserving existing environment-variable and Codex credential behavior. Ralph must execute one story per iteration, commit story changes, and keep secrets out of the repository.
goals: Let users configure one local OpenAI-compatible Images API source from the web app.
Keep environment variables effective and visible as a read-only provider source.
Show Codex account/session status without exposing OAuth token secrets.
Allow provider source priority to be reordered, with fallback only when a higher-priority source is empty or unavailable.
Preserve the existing generation, local asset persistence, Codex login, and COS backup flows.
nonGoals: Do not support multiple saved local API profiles in this task.
Do not write or edit .env files from the browser.
Do not return complete API keys, Codex access tokens, refresh tokens, or ID tokens to the browser.
Do not add a model marketplace, arbitrary provider request mapping, billing, user accounts, or cloud sync.
Do not auto-fallback to a lower-priority provider after a selected provider returns a generation error.
successMetrics: A user can open a global configuration dialog from the top-right navigation.
The dialog shows environment, local API, and Codex provider sources, including unavailable sources.
A user can save one local API key/base URL/model configuration without editing .env.
The provider source priority persists after refresh and controls image generation provider selection.
Saved secrets are masked in API responses and never logged or committed.
openQuestions: No product-level open questions remain. The task defaults are: one saved local API profile, read-only environment source, Codex account status only, fallback only for empty or unavailable sources, and default priority env > local API > Codex.

stack.frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript + tldraw + Tailwind CSS + lucide-react
stack.database: SQLite using Drizzle ORM and better-sqlite3
stack.imageProvider: OpenAI-compatible Images API plus Codex Responses image provider
stack.storage: Host ./data or DATA_DIR for SQLite, assets, COS configuration, Codex tokens, and locally saved provider secrets

routes.name: Provider Configuration
routes.purpose: Read and save provider source priority plus one local OpenAI-compatible API configuration.

routes.name: Auth Status
routes.purpose: Continue returning the active provider while adding enough source summary data for UI status.

routes.name: Text To Image
routes.purpose: Generate using the first available configured provider source by priority.

routes.name: Reference Image Generation
routes.purpose: Edit/reference generation using the first available configured provider source by priority.

uiNotes: Add the settings entry at the far right of the global top navigation so it is reachable from Home, Canvas, and Gallery.
The existing Home API setup action should open the new provider configuration dialog.
Use the existing warm paper, terracotta, teal, restrained utility-panel design language.
The dialog should prioritize an efficient operational settings surface over marketing copy.
Show unavailable sources in the priority list so users understand why fallback happens.
Use drag-and-drop for mouse users and accessible up/down controls for keyboard users.
Keep all secret fields masked on readback; API keys can be replaced by entering a new value.
rules: Ralph must complete exactly one story per iteration and commit story changes unless explicitly run with no-commit.
Use pnpm install if dependencies are missing; the package manager is pinned to pnpm@9.14.2.
Run pnpm typecheck and pnpm build before completing each story.
Frontend stories must perform browser verification against the running app.
Keep local agent scratch files under .codex-temp/ and do not commit .ralph, .env, data, generated images, SQLite databases, or build output.
Secrets must only be read from .env, runtime environment, Codex token storage, or the new local SQLite provider config; secrets must never be logged.
Environment variables remain a read-only source in the UI and must not be written by the app.
The default source priority is env-openai, local-openai, codex.
Provider fallback occurs only when a source is empty, disabled by absence of required credentials, or unavailable before request creation.
A provider selected for a generation request must not silently fallback after an upstream generation error.
Codex token values must not be returned to the browser; show only account/session status such as email, account ID, expiry, refresh time, and unavailable reason.

stories.title: Add provider configuration storage and priority selection
stories.description: As a local app user, I want provider credentials and priority to be managed by the backend so environment variables, one saved local API configuration, and Codex can participate in a predictable fallback chain.
acceptanceCriteria: Add shared contracts for provider configuration responses, save requests, provider source IDs, provider source kinds, and masked secrets.
Add a SQLite-backed provider configuration store with one active row for source order and one local OpenAI-compatible API configuration.
Initialize or migrate the provider config table at startup using the existing database.ts style.
Implement GET /api/provider-config returning env OpenAI, local OpenAI, and Codex sources in saved priority order, including unavailable sources.
Implement PUT /api/provider-config to save source order and one local API configuration with API key replacement/preservation semantics.
Environment source details are read from OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_BASE_URL, OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL, and OPENAI_IMAGE_TIMEOUT_MS; API key is masked, while Base URL, model, and timeout are visible.
Local API source supports API key, Base URL, model advanced field, and timeout with sensible defaults matching the existing provider defaults.
Update /api/auth/status to preserve current compatibility while exposing the active source summary needed by the UI.
Update createConfiguredImageProvider to select the first available source by priority: env OpenAI, local OpenAI, or Codex according to saved order.
Do not fallback after a selected provider returns an upstream generation error; return the existing provider error flow.
Negative case: empty local API key is treated as unavailable and falls through to the next source without saving an unusable active provider.
Negative case: malformed source order or unsupported source IDs are rejected with 400 JSON errors and no secret logging.
Verification: deterministic backend checks or focused unit-level smoke scripts cover source ordering, masking, local API preservation, and fallback selection.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Build global provider configuration dialog
stories.description: As a creator, I want a top-right configuration dialog where I can see environment and Codex status, enter one local API configuration, and reorder provider priority without editing .env.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a top-right global settings button to the existing top navigation and keep it reachable from Home, Canvas, and Gallery.
Change the Home page API setup action to open the new provider configuration dialog instead of the old documentation-only modal.
Render a modal dialog with an upper provider priority list and lower source detail sections.
The priority list shows env OpenAI, local OpenAI, and Codex sources whether available or unavailable, with clear Chinese status copy.
Support pointer drag-and-drop reordering plus keyboard-accessible up/down controls, and persist the order through PUT /api/provider-config.
The environment section is read-only, masks the API key, and shows Base URL, model, timeout, and a compact .env/restart hint.
The local API section lets the user enter or replace API key, Base URL, model in an advanced field, and timeout; saved keys are masked on reload.
The Codex section shows email or account ID, availability, expiry, refresh time, unavailable reason, and existing login/logout actions without exposing token values.
After saving a non-empty local API key, refresh auth/provider status and allow the existing route guard to navigate to /canvas when a provider is available.
Keep the existing provider status popover coherent with the new active source and do not duplicate conflicting status text.
Mobile and desktop layouts must keep text within containers, controls usable, and no overlapping UI.
Update README.md, README.zh-CN.md, and .env.example to document the in-app provider configuration, environment precedence, local SQLite secret storage, and no-public-exposure guidance.

Browser verification: open dialog from Home, Canvas, and Gallery; reorder sources; save local API config; refresh and verify masked readback; verify unavailable source fallback messaging; verify responsive layout.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.
